requirements.title: Scenario Model
requirements.description: Define data shape for scenario slices, steps, choices, terminal options, and source citations.
requirements.acceptance: A scenario can express the unclear-requirements path, current step, next command options, and stop/continue routes without hardcoding UI logic.

requirements.title: Scenario Slice Navigation
requirements.description: Render one active scenario slice at a time and allow switching at key nodes.
requirements.acceptance: Users see a focused map for the active scenario and can switch to another scenario only from explicit transition points.

requirements.title: Stepwise Command Choice
requirements.description: Let users progress through command choices based on previous output conditions.
requirements.acceptance: Users can follow first command, output condition, next command choices, and reach a closing node.

requirements.title: Continuation Route Panel
requirements.description: Present stop, quality pipeline, and knowledge-capture continuation choices as secondary actions.
requirements.acceptance: Closing nodes show continuation choices in the detail panel without expanding downstream pipeline internals on the map.

requirements.title: Upstream Citation Layer
requirements.description: Attach `maestro-flow` source references to scenario facts and command sequences.
requirements.acceptance: Each command sequence can cite an upstream workflow/source reference or be marked as pending citation.

requirements.title: Validation Checklist
requirements.description: Provide internal validation prompts to confirm users can choose the command sequence.
requirements.acceptance: Users can self-check that they can choose the first command and subsequent commands until stopping or continuing.

constraints.constraint: Scenario data must be separated from React rendering logic.
constraints.rationale: Current `src/main.tsx` embeds static nodes and edges inline, which blocks scalable scenario slices.

constraints.constraint: The map must keep the recommended path primary and put alternatives, terminal routes, and source badges in the detail panel.
constraints.rationale: This avoids choice overload while preserving continuation options.

constraints.constraint: Command names and sequence semantics must align with `maestro-flow`.
constraints.rationale: The submodule is the command-chain source of truth.

domain.problem_statement: Users need to choose Maestro commands step by step from a scenario state, not receive a one-shot command recommendation.
domain.audience: Internal validators and future Maestro users learning command sequences.

terminology.definition: Data model for scenario slices, steps, choices, terminal options, transitions, and citations.
Focused workflow subset for one user scenario.
Optional exit from a closing node into stop, quality, or knowledge-capture routes.

non_goals.title: Real status parsing in MVP
non_goals.rationale: The MVP explains output conditions but does not act as an execution-state assistant.

non_goals.title: Visual design system in this brainstorm
non_goals.rationale: User selected separate visual design pass.

insights.summary: State should be minimal: activeScenarioId, activeStepId, selectedNodeId, and optional visitedStepIds.
The UX must answer where am I, why this command, what output matters, and what can I do next.
Human validation must verify command-sequence comprehension, while automated tests verify data transitions.

open_questions.question: Which exact `maestro-flow` files should cite the first scenario command sequence?
What minimum internal validation pass threshold is required before roadmap implementation is considered successful?
